mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during periods of tight monetary policy, or the mechanism reverses direction when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms macroeconomic expansion and validates corporate earnings forecasts, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 5-day returns in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.0%, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in consumer price inflation and the federal funds rate compress corporate profit margins and elevate equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 60-day returns in > 50% of observed instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when inflation remains below 2.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor demand and rising household income, which fuels consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when the unemployment rate remains above 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals contracting manufacturing output and weakening supply-side momentum, which triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when INDPRO drops > 0.5%, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods in out-of-sample tests.
